articles.title: 'That Is Really Nuts,' Says Joe Rogan After Learning Nearly 1 In 10 Credit Card Debts Default. 'One Of The Biggest Problems That Most People Face'
articles.description: Millions of Americans are falling behind on debt payments, and the latest data suggest financial stress is continuing to build. On a recent episode of the “ Joe Rogan Experience ” podcast, personal finance creator Caleb Hammer shared some eye-opening statistics that caught comedian and host Joe Rogan off guard. Hammer told Rogan that Americans currently hold about $1.6 trillion in credit card debt and that roughly 7% of credit card accounts end up in default. “That’s crazy,” Rogan replied. Rogan noticed that the figure is close to 1 in 10 accounts and responded, “That is really nuts.” Don't Miss: Paying too much toward debt? Find out how much you could save with a quick, free check. Need extra retirement cash flow? Homeowners with significant equity are exploring alternatives to traditional HELOCs. Debt Problems Are Growing Across The Board The discussion comes at a time when new data suggest Americans are facing increasing financial pressure . According to a May report by The Kobeissi Letter, serious credit card delinquencies rose to 13.1% during Q1 2026, the highest level since Q4 2010. BREAKING: Credit card serious delinquencies rose +0.4 percentage points in Q1 2026, to 13.1%, the highest since Q4 2010. This is only below the 2010 peak of 13.7%, in the aftermath of the 2008 Financial Crisis. Since Q3 2022, serious credit card delinquencies have surged +5.5... pic.twitter.com/T73WW0goYL — The Kobeissi Letter (@KobeissiLetter) May 19, 2026 The report said that serious credit card delinquencies have climbed 5.5% since Q3 2022, a larger increase than the rise seen during the years surrounding the 2008 financial crisis. Hammer also said that Americans carry more auto loan debt than credit card debt, a statistic that surprised Rogan. “Really?” Rogan asked. The Kobeissi Letter said that auto loan serious delinquencies reached a record 5.6%, while student loan serious delinquencies climbed to 10.3%, the highest level since early 2020. Trending: Explore whether your retirement strategy is optimized for income, taxes, and long-term withdrawals — take the AdviserMatch quiz today. Why Financial Education Matters Hammer’s own story mirrors the financial struggles he now covers on his YouTube show , “Financial Audit.” He told Rogan that he accumulated student ... articles.title: Software Prices See Biggest Increase On Record As AI Data Centers Absorb Global Chip Supply
articles.description: Consumer prices for computer software and accessories rose roughly 14.5% year-over-year in May, the largest annual increase in data dating back to 2000. Software and Chip Prices Hit Records The figures were reported by The Kobeissi Letter, an account on X that tracks macroeconomic data, citing the Bureau of Labor Statistics’ May Consumer Price Index report, published Wednesday. Overall, U.S. consumer prices rose 4.2% year-over-year in May, with energy costs up 23.5%, driven largely by the ongoing Iran War. The producer prices for electronic components climbed 27% year-over-year, also a record. The surge comes as hyperscalers, including Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ: MSFT ), Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN ), and Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ: GOOG ) (NASDAQ: GOOGL ) continue large-scale AI data center investments , widely reported throughout 2025 ... Full story available on Benzinga.com
